evidence: Routines and orchestration create parallel runtime seam structs that mostly wrap direct package calls without enforcing behavior (goseg/routines/runtime_seams.go:18-55,123-205; goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go:13-53,142-238).

Public entrypoints delegate to `WithRuntime` variants even when orchestration logic is identical in the default path (goseg/routines/docker.go:61-64, 123-126; goseg/routines/version.go:17-35; goseg/docker/orchestration/wireguard.go:17-20; goseg/docker/orchestration/minio.go:16-19).
Domain-specific runtime type aliases are all identical to `dockerRuntime`, so alias names do not provide real capability boundaries (goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go:137-140).
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: ['Several indirection layers are structural and not policy-driven; consolidating seam layers would reduce cognitive and test harness overhead.', 'Type aliases for domain-specific runtimes are mostly nominal and should be replaced or given distinct behavior contracts.']

findings.summary: Runtime seam abstractions mostly wrap direct calls and add indirection without adding policy
evidence: Global function vars alias concrete dependencies and are immediately re-bound into seam structs (goseg/routines/runtime_seams.go:18-55).
Runtime constructors mostly just wrap the same dependency graph with `seams.RequireSeam*` and no translation/guard logic (goseg/routines/runtime_seams.go:119-205).
Public call paths route through thin `WithRuntime` wrappers even for normal execution paths (goseg/routines/docker.go:61-64, 123-126; goseg/routines/version.go:20-36).
findings.suggestion: Consolidate seams around explicit behavior seams only: keep `RequireSeam` wiring at package boundaries for test-specific overrides, remove no-op pass-through layers, and introduce one minimal runtime interface per subsystem with grouped capabilities that are actually consumed.

findings.summary: Nominal runtime aliases hide a single implementation and mislead interface boundaries
evidence: Domain runtime aliases are type-equivalent to `dockerRuntime`, so there is no implementation or contract distinction (goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go:137-140).
`newWireguardRuntime`, `newMinIORuntime`, `newUrbitRuntime` all return the same concrete runtime (goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go:224-238).

`load...WithRuntime` functions accept those aliases but immediately operate with generic container orchestration capabilities (goseg/docker/orchestration/wireguard.go:21-22; goseg/docker/orchestration/minio.go:16-18; goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit.go:22-25).
findings.suggestion: Replace the nominal aliases with explicit dedicated runtime interfaces for each subsystem (e.g., `WireguardOps`, `MinioOps`, `UrbitOps`) and provide domain-specific constructors; only expose methods each domain truly needs, or pass a common interface plus explicit per-operation injectors.
